title: WOS API Dashboard
description: Public API resource schema for read-only WOS dashboard rollups (ADR 0082 D-15 step 6). Carries cached, count-style aggregations across cases â€” open-cases-by-lifecycle-state, SLA breach summary, recent activity. These are NOT lists: per ADR 0082 D-7, count-style reads use a cached `asOf`-stamped envelope rather than cursor pagination. The aggregation surface is closed by design â€” section literals and event-class literals are normative; vendor extensions go through the closed-with-vendor-extension `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` seam (D-12) only on the per-event taxonomy. Cross-resource shapes (`LifecycleState`) are referenced from `instance.schema.json` per D-14 â€” no inline redefinition.

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0082 D-4). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

LifecycleState.description: Closed instance lifecycle posture, projected from `instance.schema.json#/$defs/LifecycleState` (ADR 0082 D-14 â€” never redefine). Mirrors the kernel runtime `status` enum; reserved literals `active | suspended | migrating | completed | terminated | stalled` plus `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` vendor extensions.

LifecycleStateCount.description: Single (lifecycle-state, count) pair in a `LifecycleStateRollup`. The closed-map shape is expressed as an array of typed pairs rather than `additionalProperties: true` keyed by `LifecycleState` so the closed-with-vendor-extension `LifecycleState` taxonomy stays load-bearing on the wire (a `Record<LifecycleState, integer>` JSON object would lose vendor-extension validation in most consumers).

state.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension lifecycle posture this row counts.

count.description: Number of workflow processes currently in this lifecycle state, computed as of `LifecycleStateRollup.asOf`. Best-effort per ADR 0082 D-7 â€” counts on append-only logs are cached, not transactionally consistent.

LifecycleStateRollup.description: Closed map from `LifecycleState` to count, returned by `GET /api/v1/dashboard/lifecycle-rollup` and embedded in `DashboardSummary.lifecycle`. Per ADR 0082 D-7 the response is a cached `asOf`-stamped envelope rather than a cursor-paginated list â€” these are aggregate counts, not item lists. A row is present for every lifecycle state the server tracks; states with zero cases MAY be omitted at server discretion.

asOf.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp the rollup was computed (ADR 0082 D-7 cached-envelope contract; ADR 0069 wire format).

items.description: One `LifecycleStateCount` per posture present in the result. Ordered by reserved-literal declaration order, then by vendor-extension ASCII order.

SlaBreachSummary.description: Counted SLA breaches across cases in the requested range, returned by `GET /api/v1/dashboard/sla-breaches` and embedded in `DashboardSummary.slaBreaches`. SLA breach semantics (governance S10.3, S10.4) are workflow-defined; the dashboard projects the count and the timestamp envelope of breach occurrences. Per ADR 0082 D-7 the response is a cached `asOf`-stamped envelope, not a list.

asOf.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp the breach summary was computed.

count.description: Total SLA breach occurrences in the requested range. Best-effort per ADR 0082 D-7; counts on append-only logs are cached, not transactionally consistent.

earliestBreachAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the earliest SLA breach in the requested range. Omitted when `count` is zero.

latestBreachAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the latest SLA breach in the requested range. Omitted when `count` is zero.

RecentActivityEventClass.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension event-class taxonomy (ADR 0082 D-12) for `RecentActivitySummary`. Reserved literals project the WOS event families a dashboard consumer typically wants to count: case-lifecycle (`case-created | case-completed | case-terminated`), task-lifecycle (`task-created | task-submitted | task-dismissed | task-expired`), review-protocol (`review-cleared | review-flagged`; governance S4), holds (`hold-engaged | hold-released`; governance S12), delegation (`delegation-granted | delegation-revoked`; governance S11), correspondence (`correspondence-sent | correspondence-received`). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix â€” vendor-specific event classes do not break compatibility.

RecentActivityEventCount.description: Single (event-class, count) pair in a `RecentActivitySummary`. The array-of-pairs shape (rather than `additionalProperties: true` keyed by event class) preserves the closed-with-vendor-extension `RecentActivityEventClass` taxonomy on the wire.

count.description: Number of events of this class observed in the requested range, computed as of `RecentActivitySummary.asOf`. Best-effort per ADR 0082 D-7.

RecentActivitySummary.description: Counts of recent governed events grouped by `RecentActivityEventClass`, returned by `GET /api/v1/dashboard/recent-activity` and embedded in `DashboardSummary.recentActivity`. Per ADR 0082 D-7 the response is a cached `asOf`-stamped envelope rather than a cursor-paginated list â€” the dashboard surface is count-style.

asOf.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp the summary was computed.

items.description: One `RecentActivityEventCount` per event class observed in the requested range. Event classes with zero observations MAY be omitted at server discretion.

DashboardSummary.description: Top-level dashboard rollup returned by `GET /api/v1/dashboard/summary`. Composes the three section rollups (`LifecycleStateRollup`, `SlaBreachSummary`, `RecentActivitySummary`) plus a single envelope-level `asOf` timestamp so consumers can decide whether to refresh. Per ADR 0082 D-7 the response is a cached `asOf`-stamped envelope rather than a cursor-paginated list â€” counts on append-only logs are best-effort and cached aggressively at the server.

asOf.description: UTC RFC 3339 envelope-level timestamp the summary was computed (ADR 0082 D-7). Section-level `asOf` fields MAY be earlier than this when the server caches sections at different cadences.

lifecycle.description: Closed map of case counts by lifecycle posture.

slaBreaches.description: Count and timestamp envelope of SLA breaches in the requested range.

recentActivity.description: Count of recent governed events grouped by `RecentActivityEventClass`.

tenantScope.description: Deployment tenant the rollup was computed for (ADR 0068 D-1.1 â€” Proposed). Single DNS label: lowercase ASCII letter first, then lowercase letters, digits, or hyphen, length 1 to 63. Echoed from the request when supplied; resolved from `X-WOS-Tenant` otherwise. Omitted only when the server has not yet adopted ADR 0068 scope-stamping.

since.description: UTC RFC 3339 inclusive lower bound the rollup was computed against. Echoed from `DashboardRangeOptions.since` when supplied. Omitted when the server applied its default look-back window.

until.description: UTC RFC 3339 exclusive upper bound the rollup was computed against. Echoed from `DashboardRangeOptions.until` when supplied. Defaults to the server's `asOf` clock when absent.

DashboardRangeOptions.description: Query fields for `GET /api/v1/dashboard/summary`, `/lifecycle-rollup`, `/sla-breaches`, and `/recent-activity`. All fields are optional â€” a request with no parameters returns the server's default look-back window scoped to the calling actor's tenant. The contract carries no `cursor`/`limit` pagination because dashboard responses are count-style cached envelopes (ADR 0082 D-7), not lists.

since.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 lower bound on the event range used for `slaBreaches` and `recentActivity` computations (ADR 0069). When omitted the server applies a default look-back window (typically 30 days).

until.description: Exclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound on the event range. Defaults to the server's wall clock when omitted.

tenantScope.description: Deployment tenant scope filter (ADR 0068 D-1.1 â€” Proposed). MUST agree with the `X-WOS-Tenant` header when both are present; the server rejects mismatches with `WOS-1422`.
